content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 11, 1992

Shirley Wechsler
11783 Laurelcrest Drive
Studio City, CA 91604

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-325

Dear Ms. Wechsler:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of Senator David Roberti concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections
81000-91015.)

Except as noted, your letter, dated May 22, 1992, correctly
summarizes my advice concerning Senator Roberti's campaign
committee. Following his election to the 20th Senate District on
June 2, 1992, Senator Roberti may combine his campaign committee
for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for
the 20th District. However, if the election committee solicits or
receives contributions for the purpose of paying debts in
connection with the special election or special runoff election,
such contributions remain subject to the Act's contribution
limitations.

If you have any questions concerning this letter, please
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Chief, Technical Assistance
and Analysis Division

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Can Senator David Roberti combine his campaign committee for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for the 20th District following his election on June 2, 1992, and what contribution limitations apply to any debts from the special election?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Following his election to the 20th Senate District on June 2, 1992, Senator Roberti may combine his campaign committee for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for the 20th District. However, contributions solicited or received for paying debts in connection with the special election or special runoff election remain subject to the Act's contribution limitations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks traditional QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers but contains clear question and answer in letter format. Parsed question and conclusion from letter body text. Confirmation letter references prior telephone advice. File number A-92-325.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can Senator David Roberti combine his campaign committee for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for the 20th District following his election on June 2, 1992, and what contribution limitations apply to any debts from the special election?

CONCLUSION: Following his election to the 20th Senate District on June 2, 1992, Senator Roberti may combine his campaign committee for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for the 20th District. However, contributions solicited or received for paying debts in connection with the special election or special runoff election remain subject to the Act's contribution limitations.

Except as noted, your letter, dated May 22, 1992, correctly summarizes my advice concerning Senator Roberti's campaign committee. Following his election to the 20th Senate District on June 2, 1992, Senator Roberti may combine his campaign committee for the 23rd Senate District with his special election account for the 20th District. However, if the election committee solicits or receives contributions for the purpose of paying debts in connection with the special election or special runoff election, such contributions remain subject to the Act's contribution limitations. If you have any questions concerning this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel By: Carla Wardlow Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice to Senator David Roberti's representative regarding campaign disclosure provisions under the Political Reform Act, specifically permitting the combination of campaign committees following his election to the 20th Senate District while maintaining contribution limitations for special election debt payments.
